title: Voltage
short: The energy given to each unit of charge — the 'push' that drives a current, measured in volts (V).
full:
Voltage (potential difference) is the electrical energy delivered per unit of charge between two points, measured in volts: 1 V = 1 J/C. It is the 'push' that makes charge flow — water pressure is a useful analogy, with voltage as the pressure and current as the flow.

Across a resistor, voltage and current are linked by Ohm's law, U = I·R. A battery's voltage sets how much energy each coulomb of charge carries around the circuit.

The SI unit is the volt (V).
